title: Narrow, General, and Super AI
concept:
AI capability is commonly described along a spectrum of three levels, ordered by breadth and depth of cognitive ability.

**Narrow AI (Weak AI)** is the only form that exists today. It excels at one specific, well-defined task — a spam filter classifies emails, a chess engine plays chess, and an image classifier labels photos — but cannot transfer that skill to another domain. All current commercial AI products (large language models, recommendation engines, medical imaging tools) are Narrow AI.

**General AI (AGI)** is a theoretical system that would match human cognition across any intellectual task. It could reason, plan, learn, and adapt across arbitrary domains just as a person does. Whether and when AGI will be achieved remains actively debated; no AGI system exists today.

**Super AI (ASI)** would far exceed the best human performance across every intellectual domain simultaneously. It is conceived as a post-AGI stage and is entirely speculative at this point.

The progression is strictly sequential: Narrow → General → Super. Understanding this hierarchy helps testers contextualise current AI limitations and set realistic quality expectations. Narrow AI systems have well-bounded failure modes that can be systematically tested; hypothetical AGI/ASI systems raise fundamentally different safety and testing challenges.
keyPoints: Narrow AI (Weak AI) handles a single specific task and is the only form of AI that exists today.
General AI (AGI) would match human cognition across all intellectual tasks — its achievement remains uncertain and no AGI exists yet.
Super AI would far exceed human capabilities in every domain; it is purely theoretical and follows sequentially after General AI.

quiz.question: A voice assistant that can answer questions, play music, and control smart-home devices but cannot drive a car or write software code is best classified as which type of AI?
options: Super AI, because it surpasses what a single human could do in voice interaction.
General AI, because it handles multiple different tasks.
Narrow AI, because it operates within a bounded set of pre-defined tasks and cannot transfer skills to arbitrary new domains.
General AI, because it can learn new skills over time.
quiz.explanation: Even though the assistant handles several tasks, all its capabilities are pre-defined and bounded. It cannot generalise to arbitrary new domains (e.g., autonomous driving) the way a human could, so it remains Narrow AI. General AI requires unrestricted human-level cognition across all intellectual domains.
